items.correct: The sudden vertical displacement of a large volume of water, usually by an undersea earthquake, but also by underwater landslides or volcanic eruptions.
items.misconception: Tsunamis are caused by strong winds over the ocean.
items.random_wrong: Tsunamis are caused by the moon's gravity at full moon.
items.why: Students confuse tsunamis with storm surges or tidal waves. A tsunami is generated by displacement from below, not wind from above — that's why early warning relies on seismic data.

items.q: Why do glaciated valleys have a U-shape rather than a V-shape?
items.correct: Glaciers occupy the whole valley width, eroding the sides as well as the floor, producing steep sides and a flat floor (U-shape); rivers erode mainly downwards, leaving V-shape.
items.misconception: Glaciers cut U-shapes because ice is shaped like a U.
items.random_wrong: Glaciers carve V-shaped valleys just like rivers do.
items.why: The U-shape reflects the geometry of erosion (lateral plus vertical), not the shape of the ice itself. A river is confined to a thin channel and erodes vertically — a glacier fills the valley.

items.q: What is the polar jet stream?
items.correct: A fast-flowing river of air ~10km up at the boundary between the Ferrel and Polar cells, where large temperature contrasts create strong winds steering weather systems.
items.misconception: The jet stream is a low-level wind that pushes clouds along.
items.random_wrong: The jet stream is the air left behind by aircraft engines.
items.why: Jet streams sit in the upper troposphere, not at ground level. They steer mid-latitude depressions; meanders (Rossby waves) explain prolonged heatwaves, cold snaps and flooding.

items.q: Which temperature target does the Paris Agreement 'pursue efforts' to achieve?
items.why: It's 1.5 °C above pre-industrial. “1 °C above pre-industrial” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which protocol set the first binding emissions targets, entering into force 2005?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Kyoto Protocol. “Montreal Protocol” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In which year was the Paris Agreement adopted?
items.why: The real figure is 2015. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
